name: Institute for Understanding Anti-Palestinian Racism

zakat.claim_evidence: The official donation page and other organizational descriptions for the Institute for the Understanding of Anti-Palestinian Racism (IUAPR) do not explicitly mention 'zakat' or 'zakat-eligible' donations. The donation page states, "Your generous contribution will immediately impact and empower our research, education, and advocacy." There is no mention of dedicated zakat funds or campaigns. (Source: https://antipalestinianracism.org)

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects that while <cite id="4">outcomes are reported</cite> (such as faculty association rejections), there is no independent third-party evaluation.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The organization uses <cite id="2">empirical research to define the problem</cite>, which then informs <cite id="3">education and advocacy</cite> to change institutional behaviors.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that advocacy efforts are resulting in measurable policy changes within academic and public institutions.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: As a <cite id="9">newly founded organization</cite> in a niche space, it has significant capacity to absorb funds for expanding its research staff.

strengths.detail: The organization fills a niche gap by providing <cite id="1">empirical research on anti-Palestinian racism</cite>, a topic often underserved in mainstream academic and policy circles.
Successfully influenced the academic discourse, with <cite id="4">40 faculty associations</cite> adopting their research-backed frameworks regarding institutional definitions.

strengths_deep_dive: The Institute's primary strength lies in its <cite id="3">strategic advocacy and capacity building</cite>. By focusing on 'Naming, Framing, and Manifestations' of racism, they provide a <cite id="2">clear theory of change</cite> that moves from data collection to public awareness. Their research has specifically targeted institutional policies, as evidenced by their tracking of <cite id="4">faculty association rejections</cite> of restrictive definitions that impact academic freedom.
rich_narrative.summary: The Institute for the Understanding of Anti-Palestinian Racism Inc is a US-based research and advocacy organization that addresses systemic bias through <cite id="1">empirical study and public education</cite>. Operating as a non-profit entity, the Institute focuses on <cite id="2">naming and framing anti-Palestinian racism</cite> as a distinct form of prejudice. Its work is primarily divided into three pillars: <cite id="3">empirical research, education and training, and strategic advocacy</cite>. A significant portion of its early impact has been documented in the academic sphere, where the organization has tracked <cite id="4">40 faculty associations rejecting the IHRA definition</cite> of antisemitism in favor of frameworks that protect Palestinian speech. While the organization maintains a <cite id="5">digital presence for public engagement</cite>, it is currently in an emerging stage of development. As an advocacy-focused group, it provides <cite id="6">structured training programs</cite> designed to increase public awareness of the manifestations of racism. However, because the organization is in its early years, <cite id="7">publicly available financial data is currently limited</cite>, and it does not yet have a full rating from major charity evaluators. The Institute <cite id="8">accepts charitable contributions online</cite> to support its mission of documenting and challenging systemic exclusion.
